description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, tan-colored stone with a roughly triangular profile and a flat, broad face on one side. The stone's surface is mostly matte with subtle natural veining and layering, giving it a weathered, sedimentary appearance. One edge is jagged and uneven, while the opposite side is relatively straight and thin, suggesting it may have been split or cut from a larger rock. The coloration ranges from light beige to hints of reddish-brown, with some areas showing more pronounced textural roughness. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or barrier in urban landscapes such as parks, pathways, or garden borders.
description: A large, irregular tan stone with a flat face, jagged edge, and natural veining, showing signs of weathering and sedimentary layering.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing its triangular shape and subtle surface veining.
description_view_1: A thin, edge-on view reveals the stone's narrow profile and the straightness of one side.
description_view_2: The jagged, rough edge and layered texture are prominent, highlighting the stone's natural breakage.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, but with more visible surface texture and color variation.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or edge barrier
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural veining and layering, irregular and organic in appearance, with no artificial carvings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly eroded, with visible textural variation indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, often partially embedded or surrounded by vegetation.
